2025-Q3.mgmt_statement_summary: Announces Royal Beach Club Santorini as the next addition to the exclusive destination portfolio, extending brand reach beyond the ship.

status_delta.detail: first quarter this asset appears in the lifecycle record

key_uncertainties: share of $1.8B non-ship capital allocated to Santorini specifically
guest mix between city tour and beach club
2025-Q4.mgmt_statement_summary: Santorini called out as a meaningful piece of $1.8B non-ship capital alongside Cozumel Beach Club and Perfect Day Mexico; framed as complementary to (not substitute for) the Santorini city experience.

key_uncertainties: early-season guest satisfaction and rebooking signal
incremental yield vs. like-for-like Santorini calls
ramp curve relative to Royal Beach Club Paradise Island
2026-Q1.mgmt_statement_summary: Recently opened Royal Beach Club Santorini, developed with local stakeholders, positioned as the centerpiece of the 'ultimate Santorini Day' guest itinerary.

asked_by_history.framing: New-hardware vs. like-for-like contribution to yield growth, with RBC Santorini grouped alongside Star, Xcel, and Paradise Island as the new-product slate; also tied to Europe yield trajectory.

primary_framings_observed: New private destination contribution to yield growth
European itinerary differentiation and pricing power

currently_open_questions_as_of_2026Q1: What is the per-guest economics of RBC Santorini versus RBC Paradise Island in year one?
How much of 2026 Europe yield growth is attributable to Santorini vs. broader European pricing?
What is the capacity throughput of the destination and how does it scale across the Aegean season?
Will Santorini operate at the upper end of the destination ROIC range implied by Perfecta?
